summary: WaveWatch iteration 32 backend bug-fix verification: all 9 pytest tests pass. Verified register rate-limit relaxed (30/24h, 6 consecutive registers OK), activity_events properly logged for register/code_redeem/play, mark-all-watched on Breaking Bad (62 episodes) writes to watch_history and is reflected in detailed-stats with correct total_watch_time formula (movies*110 + episodes*42), platform-reviews excludes seed_user_*, /api/admin/activities returns enriched format with type/username/details/email/ip/code/code_type/title plus backward-compat admin_username/action aliases.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 3152 lines - should be split into routers/modules per code_review_guideline (>700 lines).

mark_all_episodes_watched composes episode content_id as int(f'{show_id}{snum}{ep}') which can collide across shows (e.g. show 13961 S0E0 vs show 1396 S1E0). Recommend using a tuple/string key like f'{show_id}-{snum}-{ep}' to avoid stat double-counting across series.
TMDB API key has a hardcoded fallback in mark_all_episodes_watched ('d4b8332681051181b69c8a6c9ba1a70a') - secret should not be in source.

detailed-stats fallback adds shows_watched*45 only when episodes_watched==0 globally; user with episodes from one show + another show without episodes will undercount the latter. Track per-show fallback instead.
test_credentials: Admin: admin@wavewatch.com / WaveWatch2026! (used). Test users created on the fly with @wavewatch.dev domain (.test TLD rejected by pydantic email validator).
seed_data_creation: Created several throwaway test users (test_*@wavewatch.dev, rl_*@wavewatch.dev) and one VIP activation code via admin endpoint. Test movies posted to history with TEST_ prefix. No cleanup performed.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend tests live at /app/backend/tests/test_iteration32_bugfixes.py (pytest, JUnit XML). Tests rely on .dev TLD for register because pydantic email-validator (via fastapi) rejects reserved .test TLD. Breaking Bad show_id=1396 returns 62 episodes via TMDB. The /api/admin/activities endpoint deduplicates fields with both new (username/details) and legacy (admin_username/action) aliases.
rca of the issue: Initial test run failed with 422 'reserved special-use TLD' because pydantic EmailStr blocks @*.test addresses. Switched to @wavewatch.dev to get past validation; all bug-fix assertions then passed.
